title: Master Hermes Desktop in 34 mins
summary: A tutorial on setting up and using Hermes Desktop, a control center for managing AI agents with features including local LLM integration, VPS deployment, remote gateway access via Tailscale, profiles, sessions, sub-agents, and cron blueprints. The video demonstrates switching from Telegram-based control to a dedicated desktop application for better workflow organization.
writeup: This tutorial walks through setting up and operating Hermes Desktop, a control center for managing AI agents, covering local LLM integration, VPS deployment, remote gateway access via Tailscale, profiles, sessions, sub-agents, and cron blueprints. It frames the move from Telegram-based control to a dedicated desktop application as a way to organize agent workflows. The takeaways center on self-hosted agent orchestration: running models locally, deploying to a VPS, reaching a remote gateway over Tailscale, and scheduling work through cron blueprints.
